input.topic: Executive Briefing Room scene for an Arika brand film

recommendation.summary: Content brief requests a video-format scene set in the Executive Briefing Room for an Arika brand film. Per Design's storyboard-before-generation doctrine, I've produced a complete 7-field storyboard (hook, visual, camera, voice, music, duration, prompt) shaped by Content's Story Architecture sequencing (Problem → Insight → Demonstration → Framework → Proof → Action). The requested environment maps cleanly to a named Creative Digital Twin room — Executive Briefing Room (podcast) — so no new environment concept is invented and no flag to the Brand & Environment Consistency Checker is needed. The storyboard is ready to hand off to the Production Engine Coordinator; no generation should start before this handoff.
recommendedActions: Confirm the underlying content brief exists in Content (04)'s Notion database with all required fields (Objective, Pillar, Persona, Script, Visual Direction) before finalizing the storyboard prompt
Validate the storyboard against the Executive Briefing Room persistent-room spec so the brand film stays environment-consistent across all Arika assets
Enforce the no-visible-AI-artifacts / human-realistic bar in the generation prompt and note it as the acceptance criterion
Hand the completed 7-field storyboard to the Production Engine Coordinator and block any generation until handoff is acknowledged
If additional scenes in the brand film use other environments, storyboard each separately and confirm each maps to a named Creative Digital Twin room
approvalReasons: Brand film is a high-visibility flagship asset; a human should sign off on the storyboard before generation given the storyboard-is-the-durable-asset doctrine
Storyboard should be reconciled against the actual Content (04) brief fields, which were not fully supplied in this trigger input

storyboard.hook: 'Most executive briefings inform. The best ones change the decision in the room.' — cold open on a single decisive statement before Arika is named.
storyboard.visual: Executive Briefing Room (named Creative Digital Twin — podcast environment). Warm-neutral acoustic-panel walls, a matte conference table, subtle Arika brand accent lighting, a single executive presenter seated across from an implied listener. Clean, premium, human-realistic finish — no visible AI artifacts. Follows Story Architecture: open on the Problem (decisions made on noise), reveal the Insight (clarity beats volume), Demonstration on-table, resolve to Action.
storyboard.camera: Slow 4s push-in from a wide establishing shot of the room to a chest-up medium of the presenter; single subtle rack focus onto the presenter's eyes at the Insight beat; locked-off medium for the closing Action line.
storyboard.voice: Confident, measured executive VO — warm authority, unhurried pacing, human cadence with natural micro-pauses. Female or male, low-mid register; no synthetic tonality.
storyboard.music: Restrained cinematic underscore — soft sustained pads with a single rising piano motif at the Insight beat, resolving to a warm sustained chord under the closing line. Low bed, never competing with VO.
storyboard.prompt: Cinematic brand-film scene inside a premium Executive Briefing Room: warm-neutral acoustic-panel walls, matte conference table, subtle Arika accent lighting, one poised executive presenter seated mid-conversation. Slow 4-second dolly push from wide establishing to chest-up medium, single soft rack focus to the presenter's eyes at the insight beat, locked-off medium for the final line. Photoreal, human-realistic skin and materials, natural volumetric lighting, shallow depth of field, no visible AI artifacts, premium documentary-grade grade. 20s, 16:9. Narrative arc: problem statement → insight reveal → tabletop demonstration → decisive call to action.
